summary.narrative: On his Better Kentucky Plan infrastructure pledges (broadband, water/wastewater, school facilities), Beshear secured the targeted appropriations and exceeded several, with funding signed into law and administered statewide. He delivered on medical marijuana legalization and restoration of voting rights for nonviolent offenders, and advanced major transportation projects (Mountain Parkway nearing completion; I-69 crossing still pre-construction). His universal pre-K pledge stalled against a Republican supermajority that declined to fund it, leading him to pursue a two-county executive pilot, and his stated opposition to I-69 tolls was overridden by the legislature.
summary.grade_rationale: The record shows a high rate of full delivery on well-specified, quantitative infrastructure promises and several clearly delivered qualitative actions within executive authority, balanced by stalled or partially delivered items (universal pre-K, I-69 bridge) where a non-aligned legislature was a necessary co-actor. Promise quality is generally high (clarity 3-5, statewide scale), and delivery clusters at D4 with documented action, with the lower buckets driven by external legislative blockage rather than inaction.

what_happened: April 2021: HB 320/HB 382 signed into law allocating $300 million in federal ARP dollars to broadband (c122)
Accomplishments record cites $2 billion+ secured for high-speed internet to date (c92)

data_gaps: Several Better Kentucky Plan appropriations originated in 2021/2022 (prior term) but are carried as ongoing accomplishments; reviewer should confirm term attribution where delivery spans both terms. I-69 bridge promise was originally framed as a 'first term' pledge (c16) but reappears as ongoing; time_pressure left null throughout because promises lack explicit single-term deadlines. Military Family Trust Fund expansion rests on a single official source (LIMITED EVIDENCE). Accomplishments figures are self-reported by the governor's office (potential CREDIT OVERCLAIMED risk not formally flagged absent contradicting evidence).

provisional_status.display: Grade is record-to-date — term in progress, 63.1% elapsed (as of 2026-06-22). It will change as the term continues.
provisional_status.note: Distinct from any per-promise 'PROVISIONAL (early-term)' flag in scorecard.card_flags, which concerns a single promise's own deadline, not the term's completeness.
